skills.summary: Vendor-neutral, governed, READ-ONLY energy/substation telecontrol data tap — the energy edition (变电/电力) of Industrial-AIOps. Monitor-direction telemetry over...
skills.description: Vendor-neutral, governed, READ-ONLY energy/substation telecontrol data tap — the energy edition (变电/电力) of Industrial-AIOps. Monitor-direction telemetry over IEC 60870-5-104 (IEC-104: RTU link status, general interrogation, single monitored-point reads by IOA — 遥测/遥信), DNP3 / IEEE 1815 (outstation link status, Class 0/1/2/3 integrity poll), and IEC 61850 MMS (IED logical-device directory, model browse, data-attribute reads) — plus the base cross-protocol brain (dataflow diagnosis, alarm bad-actors, data-quality scorecards, OEE, asset inventory, compliance self-assessment) mounted on the same server. Use when the task names IEC 60870-5-104 / IEC-104 / 104规约, DNP3 / IEEE 1815 / outstation, IEC 61850 / MMS / IED / GOOSE (GOOSE and Sampled Values are NOT supported — MMS reads only), a substation / 变电站 / RTU / SCADA gateway / telecontrol front-end, energy / power / utility SCADA telemetry, or 遥测遥信 / 电力监控. Routes to the iaiops-energy MCP server. Monitor-only by design: no control/operate (CROB, setpoints, select-before-operate, IEC-104 command ASDUs) is exposed. Do NOT use for factory/building/process protocols (OPC-UA, Modbus, S7, BACnet, MQTT …) — those live in the base iaiops server — nor for IT/network gear, Kubernetes, hypervisors, or backups.

latestVersion.changelog:
iaiops-energy 0.1.7 — Initial Release

- Introduces a vendor-neutral, audited, read-only telemetry data tap for energy/substation environments using IEC 60870-5-104, DNP3/IEEE 1815, and IEC 61850 MMS protocols.
- Supports monitor-direction (read-only) data collection: no control or operate actions are exposed by design.
- Includes protocol-specific tools for RTU/IED link status, point reads, general interrogations, and integrity polls.
- Integrates "cross-protocol brain" analytics, diagnostics, asset inventory, and compliance self-assessment features from the base iaiops package.
- Clear separation from protocols and use cases outside substation/utility telemetry (e.g., factory/process protocols), which are routed to the base iaiops server.
- Honest verification matrix provided: IEC 104 monitor path is CI-verified; DNP3 and IEC 61850 are manually tested with live round-trips.

- Provides step-by-step guidance, including searching issues/PRs, checking for fixes, and when to escalate.

skills.description: Generate spoken voiceover and narration from a script, one speaker, with control over emotion, pacing, and voice. Use when the user says "read this in a warm voice", "narrate my video", "voiceover for this ad", "turn this script into audio", "audiobook narration", "an IVR or phone greeting", or wants text-to-speech with a specific delivery. For two speakers trading turns, use dialogue-audio. To clone a specific voice or design a new one from a description, use voice-cloning. To attach the result to a video, use add-audio-to-video or talking-avatar.

latestVersion.changelog:
- Initial release of the voiceover skill for generating natural spoken narration from a script.
- Supports detailed control over emotion, pacing, emphasis, and voice via inline bracket direction tags.
- Integrates multiple TTS models (Fish Audio S2.1 Pro, Inworld Realtime TTS-2, Qwen3-TTS CustomVoice, Dia2 2B) with model-specific features and field mapping.
- Provides instructions for voice selection, delivery briefs, and how to match tone to use cases (ads, audiobooks, IVR, etc).
- Documents how to add paralanguage cues, pronounce special words, and handle text normalization for numbers, dates, and abbreviations.
- Outlines clear distinctions between this skill and others (dialogue-audio for multi-speaker, voice-cloning for custom voices, etc).
